standards.title: Pharmacy Organization and Formulary Management
standards.description: The organization maintains a structured pharmacy department with a defined formulary that is periodically reviewed and updated.

objective_elements.description: A hospital formulary is established, approved by the pharmacy and therapeutics committee, and made available to prescribers.

measurable_elements.description: The organization maintains an approved formulary developed by the pharmacy and therapeutics committee, accessible to all prescribing staff.

objective_elements.description: The formulary undergoes periodic review at defined intervals with documented additions, deletions, and rationale.

measurable_elements.description: Evidence exists that the formulary is reviewed at least annually with documented minutes of the committee reviewing additions and deletions.

objective_elements.description: The pharmacy department is organized with defined leadership, adequate staffing, and clear reporting structure.

measurable_elements.description: The pharmacy department has a designated head pharmacist with defined responsibilities, adequate qualified staff, and an organizational chart.

objective_elements.description: Policies and procedures governing all aspects of medication management are documented and accessible to relevant staff.

measurable_elements.description: Comprehensive medication management policies and procedures are documented, approved by competent authority, and accessible to pharmacy and clinical staff.

objective_elements.description: A process exists for obtaining medications not available in the formulary, including emergency and after-hours procurement.

measurable_elements.description: A documented process addresses obtaining non-formulary medications, including authorization requirements and after-hours availability.

objective_elements.description: The pharmacy and therapeutics committee includes multidisciplinary representation and meets at defined intervals.

measurable_elements.description: The pharmacy and therapeutics committee has representation from medical, nursing, pharmacy, and administration departments and meets at least quarterly with documented minutes.

objective_elements.description: Drug utilization reviews and pharmacoeconomic analyses are conducted periodically to optimize formulary selections.

measurable_elements.description: Periodic drug utilization reviews and cost-effectiveness analyses are conducted and results used to refine formulary composition.

objective_elements.description: Antibiotic stewardship principles are integrated into the formulary management process.

measurable_elements.description: The organization has antibiotic stewardship guidelines integrated into formulary management with restricted antibiotic prescribing protocols.

standards.title: Medication Storage and Security
standards.description: Medications are stored safely and securely under conditions that ensure their integrity, potency, and safety.

objective_elements.description: All medication storage areas maintain appropriate environmental conditions including temperature, humidity, and light control.

measurable_elements.description: Medication storage areas are maintained at appropriate temperature and humidity levels with continuous monitoring and documented logs.

objective_elements.description: Controlled substances and narcotics are stored in double-locked cabinets with strict access controls and accountability records.

measurable_elements.description: Narcotics and controlled substances are stored under double lock with access restricted to authorized personnel and shift-wise accountability maintained.

objective_elements.description: Cold chain medications are stored in dedicated refrigeration units with temperature excursion monitoring and action protocols.

measurable_elements.description: Cold chain medications are stored in dedicated pharmaceutical-grade refrigerators with twice-daily temperature recording and documented action plans for excursions.

objective_elements.description: A systematic process ensures expired, damaged, or recalled medications are identified, segregated, and disposed of safely.

measurable_elements.description: Regular checks identify near-expiry, expired, damaged, and recalled medications which are segregated, documented, and disposed of per regulatory requirements.

objective_elements.description: Medication storage areas outside the pharmacy, including nursing stations and operating rooms, are subject to the same storage standards.

measurable_elements.description: Medication storage at ward level, nursing stations, and procedure areas follows the same security, temperature, and labeling standards as the central pharmacy.

objective_elements.description: Radioactive, cytotoxic, and other hazardous medications are stored separately with appropriate safety measures.

measurable_elements.description: Hazardous medications including cytotoxic agents and radioactive materials are stored in designated areas with safety equipment and handling protocols.

objective_elements.description: Automated storage and tracking technologies are employed to enhance medication inventory accuracy and security.

measurable_elements.description: The organization utilizes automated dispensing cabinets or inventory tracking systems to improve medication storage accuracy and reduce discrepancies.

standards.title: Medication Procurement and Inventory Control
standards.description: The organization has a defined process for procuring medications ensuring quality, availability, and regulatory compliance.

objective_elements.description: Medication procurement follows a documented policy ensuring sourcing from licensed and approved vendors.

measurable_elements.description: The organization procures medications only from licensed suppliers with a documented vendor qualification and approval process.

objective_elements.description: An inventory management system tracks medication stock levels, consumption patterns, and reorder points.

measurable_elements.description: A systematic inventory management process with defined minimum and maximum stock levels, reorder points, and consumption tracking is implemented.

objective_elements.description: Incoming medications are inspected for integrity, proper labeling, expiry date, and batch details upon receipt.

measurable_elements.description: Each medication consignment is inspected upon receipt for packaging integrity, correct labeling, batch number, manufacturing and expiry dates.

objective_elements.description: Drug shortage management protocols exist to ensure continuity of patient care when specific medications are unavailable.

measurable_elements.description: The organization has protocols for managing drug shortages including therapeutic alternatives, communication to prescribers, and emergency procurement channels.

objective_elements.description: Drug recall procedures are documented and implemented promptly when manufacturer or regulatory recalls are issued.

measurable_elements.description: A documented drug recall procedure ensures rapid identification, quarantine, and return of recalled medications with traceability maintained.

objective_elements.description: Periodic physical stock verification is conducted and discrepancies are investigated and documented.

measurable_elements.description: Physical stock verification is conducted at defined intervals with discrepancy investigation, root cause analysis, and corrective actions documented.

standards.title: Prescribing and Transcription of Medications
standards.description: Medication orders are written clearly, completely, and in accordance with organizational policies to minimize prescribing errors.

objective_elements.description: Prescribing guidelines define who is authorized to prescribe, essential elements of a prescription, and use of approved abbreviations.

measurable_elements.description: The organization defines authorized prescribers, mandatory prescription elements, approved abbreviation lists, and prohibits dangerous abbreviations.

objective_elements.description: Prescriptions are legible or electronically generated with all mandatory elements including patient identification, drug name, dose, route, and frequency.

measurable_elements.description: All medication orders contain patient identifiers, generic drug name, dose, route, frequency, duration, prescriber signature, and are legible or computer-generated.

objective_elements.description: Verbal and telephone medication orders follow a defined read-back and verify protocol with timely co-signature.

measurable_elements.description: Verbal and telephone orders follow a read-back verification process and are co-signed by the prescriber within the organizationally defined time frame.

objective_elements.description: Patient allergy and adverse reaction history is documented and verified before prescribing any medication.

measurable_elements.description: Allergy status and prior adverse drug reaction history are documented prominently in the medical record and verified before each new prescription.

objective_elements.description: Drug-drug interactions and therapeutic duplications are screened at the point of prescribing or order entry.

measurable_elements.description: A mechanism exists to screen for clinically significant drug-drug interactions, drug-food interactions, and therapeutic duplications at the time of prescribing.

objective_elements.description: Dose adjustment guidelines are available for special populations including pediatric, geriatric, renal, and hepatic impairment patients.

measurable_elements.description: Dosage adjustment references and guidelines are available for pediatric, geriatric, and organ-impaired patients and are used during prescribing.

objective_elements.description: Medication order transcription processes include verification steps to prevent transcription errors.

measurable_elements.description: Transcription of medication orders includes a verification step by a second qualified person and any transcription errors are documented and analyzed.

standards.title: Dispensing of Medications
standards.description: Medications are dispensed accurately and safely by qualified personnel with appropriate labeling and patient counseling.

objective_elements.description: Medications are dispensed only by qualified and authorized pharmacy personnel following verification of the prescription.

measurable_elements.description: Dispensing is performed only by licensed pharmacists or trained pharmacy technicians under pharmacist supervision with prescription verification before dispensing.

objective_elements.description: Dispensed medications are labeled accurately with patient name, drug name, strength, dose, route, frequency, and expiry information.

measurable_elements.description: Each dispensed medication carries a label with patient identification, drug name and strength, dose instructions, route, frequency, quantity, and expiry date.

objective_elements.description: Patient counseling on medication use, precautions, and potential side effects is provided at the time of dispensing.

measurable_elements.description: Pharmacists provide patient counseling at the point of dispensing covering proper use, storage, common side effects, and when to seek medical advice.

objective_elements.description: After-hours and emergency medication dispensing processes ensure safe and timely access to essential medications.

measurable_elements.description: Defined processes for after-hours medication dispensing include night pharmacy services, floor stock management, or on-call pharmacist availability.

objective_elements.description: Unit dose or patient-specific dispensing systems are implemented to reduce medication errors.

measurable_elements.description: The organization implements unit dose dispensing or individualized patient medication systems to reduce handling errors and facilitate accurate administration.

objective_elements.description: Barcode verification or technology-assisted dispensing is used to enhance accuracy of the dispensing process.

measurable_elements.description: Technology-assisted dispensing such as barcode scanning or automated dispensing cabinets is used to verify correct medication, dose, and patient match.

standards.title: Administration of Medications
standards.description: Medications are administered safely to patients by qualified personnel following established verification and documentation protocols.

objective_elements.description: Medications are administered following verification of the five rights: right patient, right drug, right dose, right route, and right time.

measurable_elements.description: Staff administering medications verify the five rights (patient, drug, dose, route, time) before every administration, with documentation of compliance.

objective_elements.description: Medication administration is documented in the patient record immediately after administration, including time, dose, and route.

measurable_elements.description: Each medication administration is documented in the patient medication administration record with the time, dose, route, and administering staff signature.

objective_elements.description: Intravenous medication preparation and administration follows defined protocols for dilution, rate, and compatibility.

measurable_elements.description: Protocols for IV medication preparation specify dilution standards, infusion rates, compatibility checks, and labeling requirements for prepared IV admixtures.

objective_elements.description: Self-administration of medications by patients is permitted only after competency assessment and under defined conditions.

measurable_elements.description: A policy governs patient self-administration of medications with competency assessment criteria, physician approval, and nursing oversight requirements.

objective_elements.description: Timeliness of medication administration is monitored, with critical and time-sensitive medications identified and prioritized.

measurable_elements.description: The organization monitors timely medication administration, defines acceptable time windows, and identifies time-critical medications requiring strict scheduling.

objective_elements.description: Infusion pump programming and smart pump technology safeguards are utilized for high-risk intravenous medications.

measurable_elements.description: Smart infusion pumps with dose error reduction software and drug libraries are used for high-risk IV medications with compliance data monitored.

objective_elements.description: Medication administration competency of nursing staff is assessed and documented periodically.

measurable_elements.description: Nursing staff competency in medication administration including IV admixture, high-risk drugs, and pediatric dosing is assessed at orientation and periodically thereafter.

standards.title: Monitoring of Medication Effects and Adverse Drug Reactions
standards.description: The organization monitors patient responses to medications and has a system for reporting and analyzing adverse drug reactions and medication errors.

objective_elements.description: An adverse drug reaction reporting and monitoring system is established and operational across the organization.

measurable_elements.description: The organization has a formal ADR reporting system with standardized forms, defined reporting timelines, and a process for regulatory notification.

objective_elements.description: Patients on high-risk medications, narrow therapeutic index drugs, and new medications are monitored with defined clinical parameters.

measurable_elements.description: Clinical monitoring parameters are defined for high-risk medications, narrow therapeutic index drugs, and newly introduced medications with documentation of monitoring outcomes.

objective_elements.description: A medication error reporting system encourages voluntary and non-punitive reporting of errors and near-misses.

measurable_elements.description: A non-punitive medication error and near-miss reporting system is in place that encourages voluntary reporting with documented analysis and corrective actions.

objective_elements.description: Reported adverse drug reactions and medication errors are analyzed for trends, contributing factors, and system improvements.

measurable_elements.description: ADR and medication error data are analyzed periodically for trends, root causes, and contributing factors with improvement actions tracked to completion.

objective_elements.description: Staff are trained and aware of adverse drug reaction identification, documentation, and reporting procedures.

measurable_elements.description: All clinical staff receive training on ADR identification, classification, documentation, and reporting procedures with training records maintained.

objective_elements.description: A pharmacovigilance program links internal ADR data to national pharmacovigilance databases and regulatory reporting.

measurable_elements.description: The organization participates in the national pharmacovigilance program with documented submission of serious ADRs to regulatory authorities.

standards.title: High-Alert Medication Safety
standards.description: The organization identifies high-alert medications and implements additional safeguards to prevent errors associated with their use.

objective_elements.description: A list of high-alert medications is defined, approved, and disseminated across the organization.

measurable_elements.description: The organization maintains an approved, current list of high-alert medications based on published evidence and internal incident data, accessible to all clinical staff.

objective_elements.description: High-alert medications are stored separately with distinctive labeling, warning signs, and restricted access where appropriate.

measurable_elements.description: High-alert medications are stored in designated locations with prominent warning labels, color-coded alerts, and separated from regular stock to prevent selection errors.

objective_elements.description: An independent double-check process is mandated before administration of high-alert medications.

measurable_elements.description: An independent double-check by two qualified staff members is required before dispensing or administering high-alert medications with documentation of both verifiers.

objective_elements.description: Concentrated electrolytes and other high-risk solutions are not kept in patient care areas unless clinically necessary with safeguards.

measurable_elements.description: Concentrated electrolytes are removed from patient care units or, where clinically necessary, stored with prominent warnings and standardized dilution protocols.

objective_elements.description: Standardized concentrations, dose limits, and infusion rate charts are established for high-alert medications.

measurable_elements.description: Standardized concentration charts, maximum dose limits, and infusion rate references are developed and available at points of prescribing, dispensing, and administration.

objective_elements.description: Staff competency for handling high-alert medications is assessed at induction and periodically with documented evidence.

measurable_elements.description: Competency assessment for staff handling high-alert medications is conducted at orientation and at defined intervals with remedial training for identified gaps.

standards.title: Look-Alike Sound-Alike Medication Safety
standards.description: The organization identifies and manages look-alike sound-alike medications to prevent selection and dispensing errors.

objective_elements.description: A list of look-alike sound-alike medications relevant to the organization is maintained and regularly updated.

measurable_elements.description: An organization-specific LASA list is developed based on published references and internal error data, reviewed at least annually, and disseminated to all relevant staff.

objective_elements.description: Tall man lettering and other visual differentiation strategies are used for look-alike sound-alike medications.

measurable_elements.description: Tall man lettering, color-coded labels, or other visual differentiation methods are applied to LASA medications in storage, dispensing, and electronic systems.

objective_elements.description: Look-alike sound-alike medications are stored separately or with physical barriers to prevent mix-ups.

measurable_elements.description: LASA medications are physically separated in storage areas, stored in non-adjacent locations, or segregated by shelf dividers with alert tags.

objective_elements.description: Electronic prescribing and dispensing systems include alerts for look-alike sound-alike medication pairs.

measurable_elements.description: Computerized prescribing and pharmacy systems incorporate LASA alerts that prompt verification when a LASA pair medication is selected.

objective_elements.description: Periodic audits and failure mode analyses are conducted for LASA medication processes with improvement actions tracked.

measurable_elements.description: Periodic audits of LASA safeguards are conducted with failure mode analysis, and improvement actions are documented and tracked to completion.

standards.title: Patient's Own Medications and Sample Medications
standards.description: The organization has policies governing the use, verification, storage, and documentation of patient's own medications and pharmaceutical samples.

objective_elements.description: A policy governs whether and how patient's own medications may be used during hospitalization.

measurable_elements.description: A documented policy defines conditions under which patient's own medications may be used, including physician authorization and pharmacist verification requirements.

objective_elements.description: Patient's own medications brought into the hospital are identified, verified for authenticity and integrity, and stored safely.

measurable_elements.description: Patient's own medications are identified by the pharmacist, verified for product integrity and expiry, labeled, and stored securely in the patient care area.

objective_elements.description: Sample medications are managed under a documented policy covering receipt, storage, dispensing, and tracking.

measurable_elements.description: A policy defines the management of pharmaceutical samples including authorized receipt, storage conditions, dispensing documentation, and expiry tracking.

objective_elements.description: Documentation of patient's own medications and sample medications is maintained in the patient medical record.

measurable_elements.description: Use of patient's own medications and pharmaceutical samples is documented in the patient record including name, dose, frequency, and duration of use.

objective_elements.description: Outcome tracking and effectiveness evaluation of sample medication usage is conducted to inform future procurement decisions.

measurable_elements.description: The organization tracks outcomes of sample medication use and evaluates effectiveness data to support evidence-based formulary and procurement decisions.

standards.title: Emergency Medication Management
standards.description: Emergency medications and supplies are available, standardized, and maintained for immediate use in life-threatening situations.

objective_elements.description: Emergency medications are available at defined locations throughout the organization for immediate access during emergencies.

measurable_elements.description: Emergency medications are stocked at all designated resuscitation points and critical care areas with a standardized list approved by the medical staff.

objective_elements.description: Crash carts and emergency medication kits are standardized across the organization with uniform contents and layout.

measurable_elements.description: Crash carts are standardized across the organization with a uniform contents list, drawer layout, quantity specifications, and content checklist.

objective_elements.description: Emergency medication stocks are checked at defined frequencies for completeness, expiry, and integrity with documented logs.

measurable_elements.description: Emergency medication and crash cart checks are performed at defined frequencies, with daily integrity checks and periodic full content verification against the standard list.

objective_elements.description: Tamper-evident seals or locking mechanisms are used on emergency medication kits to ensure security and integrity.

measurable_elements.description: Emergency medication kits and crash carts use tamper-evident seals with documented seal numbers, and a process ensures replenishment and resealing after each use.

objective_elements.description: Simulation-based training and drills are conducted to ensure staff competency in emergency medication retrieval and administration.

measurable_elements.description: Regular simulation drills test staff ability to locate and administer emergency medications with debriefing, gap identification, and corrective training documented.

standards.title: Patient Rights
standards.description: The organization respects and protects patient rights and communicates them effectively to patients, families, and staff.

objective_elements.description: A patient rights charter is defined, approved by leadership, and displayed prominently throughout the organization.

measurable_elements.description: The organization has an approved patient rights charter displayed in patient care areas, reception, and admissions in the local language and English.

objective_elements.description: Patient rights are communicated to patients and families at the time of admission and during the care process.

measurable_elements.description: Patient rights are communicated verbally and in writing to patients and families at admission, with documented acknowledgment of receipt.

objective_elements.description: Patient rights information is available in accessible formats including multiple languages and pictorial representations for diverse populations.

measurable_elements.description: Patient rights materials are available in the predominant languages of the community served, with pictorial formats for patients with literacy challenges.

objective_elements.description: Patients have the right to receive information about their diagnosis, treatment plan, expected outcomes, and associated costs.

measurable_elements.description: Patients are informed about their diagnosis, proposed treatment options, expected outcomes, associated risks, and estimated costs in a language they understand.

objective_elements.description: Patients have the right to refuse treatment or withdraw consent and are informed about the consequences of such decisions.

measurable_elements.description: The organization respects the patient's right to refuse or discontinue treatment, with documented counseling about consequences and patient acknowledgment.

objective_elements.description: The organization has mechanisms to protect patients from physical abuse, neglect, and harassment.

measurable_elements.description: Policies and processes protect patients from abuse, neglect, and harassment with defined reporting mechanisms, investigation procedures, and corrective actions.

objective_elements.description: Cultural, religious, spiritual, and psychosocial values of patients are respected and accommodated in the care process.

measurable_elements.description: Staff are trained to respect and accommodate patients' cultural, religious, spiritual, and psychosocial needs with documented examples of accommodations provided.

objective_elements.description: Periodic audits assess compliance with patient rights policies and results inform quality improvement initiatives.

measurable_elements.description: Periodic audits of patient rights compliance are conducted with findings analyzed, improvement actions planned, and results reported to leadership.

standards.title: Informed Consent
standards.description: The organization ensures informed consent is obtained from patients or their legal representatives before procedures, treatments, and interventions.

objective_elements.description: An informed consent policy defines the types of procedures and treatments requiring consent, the consent process, and documentation standards.

measurable_elements.description: A comprehensive informed consent policy specifies procedures requiring consent, elements of valid consent, authorized consent-takers, and documentation requirements.

objective_elements.description: Informed consent includes explanation of the procedure, risks, benefits, alternatives, and the right to refuse in a language the patient understands.

measurable_elements.description: The consent process includes explanation of the procedure, expected benefits, potential risks, available alternatives, and right to refuse, communicated in the patient's preferred language.

objective_elements.description: Separate informed consent is obtained for surgical procedures, anesthesia, blood and blood product transfusions, and high-risk treatments.

measurable_elements.description: Separate consent forms are obtained for surgery, anesthesia, blood transfusion, and other high-risk procedures with specific risks detailed for each.

objective_elements.description: Consent for anesthesia is obtained by the anesthesiologist explaining the type, risks, and alternatives of anesthesia.

measurable_elements.description: Anesthesia consent is obtained by the anesthesiologist during the pre-anesthesia assessment, documenting the type of anesthesia, specific risks, and patient queries addressed.

objective_elements.description: When the patient is unable to provide consent, consent is obtained from a legally authorized representative as defined by applicable law.

measurable_elements.description: When patients cannot consent, the organization obtains consent from legally authorized representatives with documentation of the relationship and authority.

objective_elements.description: General consent at admission covers routine care, and specific consent is obtained for each additional procedure or investigation as required.

measurable_elements.description: A general consent at admission covers routine care activities, while specific informed consent is obtained for each invasive procedure, investigation, or treatment beyond routine care.

objective_elements.description: The quality of informed consent documentation is audited periodically to ensure completeness and compliance with policy.

measurable_elements.description: Periodic audits review informed consent documentation for completeness, legibility, appropriate timing, and presence of all required elements with corrective actions for gaps.

standards.title: Privacy and Confidentiality
standards.description: The organization ensures the privacy of patients during care and maintains confidentiality of patient health information.

objective_elements.description: A comprehensive privacy and confidentiality policy governs the protection of patient information across all settings.

measurable_elements.description: The organization has an approved privacy and confidentiality policy that defines the scope of protected health information, access controls, and breach response procedures.

objective_elements.description: Physical privacy of patients is maintained during examinations, procedures, and personal care activities.

measurable_elements.description: Physical privacy is ensured through curtains, screens, private consultation rooms, and protocols for maintaining patient modesty during examinations and procedures.

objective_elements.description: Patient medical records and health information are accessible only to authorized personnel on a need-to-know basis.

measurable_elements.description: Access to patient medical records is restricted to authorized clinical and administrative staff based on role-based access controls with audit trail monitoring.

objective_elements.description: Protocols govern sharing of patient information with third parties, insurers, and external agencies with patient authorization.

measurable_elements.description: Patient information is shared with third parties, insurers, and external agencies only with documented patient authorization or as required by law, with records of disclosures maintained.

objective_elements.description: Electronic health information is protected through cybersecurity measures, encryption, and access controls.

measurable_elements.description: Electronic patient data is protected by password controls, encryption, firewall protections, and regular security assessments with documented cybersecurity protocols.

objective_elements.description: A privacy breach management process includes detection, notification, investigation, and corrective action procedures.

measurable_elements.description: The organization has a documented privacy breach response plan covering detection, containment, patient notification, investigation, and corrective actions with lessons learned.

objective_elements.description: All staff receive training on patient privacy and confidentiality at induction and through periodic refresher programs.

measurable_elements.description: Staff training on privacy and confidentiality is conducted at induction and refreshed annually with documentation of attendance and competency verification.

standards.title: Grievance Redressal
standards.description: The organization has a structured mechanism to receive, address, and resolve patient and family complaints and grievances.

objective_elements.description: A documented grievance redressal mechanism is established with clear processes for complaint registration, investigation, and resolution.

measurable_elements.description: The organization has a documented grievance redressal policy with defined processes for complaint intake, classification, investigation, resolution, and communication of outcomes.

objective_elements.description: Multiple channels are available for patients and families to register complaints, including verbal, written, and electronic options.

measurable_elements.description: Complaint registration is facilitated through multiple channels including suggestion boxes, helpline numbers, email, web portals, and direct submission to designated staff.

objective_elements.description: Complaints are acknowledged within a defined time frame and resolved within specified turnaround times.

measurable_elements.description: Complaints are acknowledged within a defined time frame, and resolution targets are established with monitoring of compliance against turnaround time benchmarks.

objective_elements.description: An escalation matrix ensures unresolved complaints are elevated to appropriate authority levels for resolution.

measurable_elements.description: A defined escalation matrix routes unresolved complaints through progressively higher authority levels with time-bound escalation triggers and documentation.

objective_elements.description: Patient and family feedback is actively solicited through structured mechanisms such as rounding and feedback forms.

measurable_elements.description: The organization actively solicits patient feedback through bedside feedback forms, discharge surveys, leadership rounding, and focused group discussions.

objective_elements.description: Complaint data is analyzed for trends, root causes, and systemic issues to drive quality improvement.

measurable_elements.description: Complaint data is analyzed periodically for trends, recurring issues, and systemic causes with improvement actions planned, implemented, and tracked for effectiveness.

objective_elements.description: Validated patient satisfaction surveys are conducted periodically with results benchmarked and used for improvement planning.

measurable_elements.description: Standardized patient satisfaction surveys are conducted at defined intervals with results analyzed, benchmarked, and used to develop targeted improvement action plans.

standards.title: Patient and Family Education
standards.description: The organization provides structured health education to patients and families to support informed decision-making and self-management.

objective_elements.description: A patient and family education program is established with defined processes for assessing education needs and delivering education.

measurable_elements.description: A structured patient education program includes assessment of learning needs, preferred learning methods, literacy level, and barriers to learning for each patient.

objective_elements.description: Patients are educated about their diagnosis, disease process, and proposed treatment plan in terms they can understand.

measurable_elements.description: Education on diagnosis, disease process, treatment plan, and expected outcomes is provided to patients and families using clear, jargon-free language with understanding verified.

objective_elements.description: Medication education covers drug purpose, dosage, administration technique, side effects, and storage requirements.

measurable_elements.description: Patients receive medication education covering purpose, dose, frequency, administration technique, potential side effects, food interactions, and storage instructions.

objective_elements.description: Dietary guidance, lifestyle modifications, and rehabilitation instructions are provided based on the patient's clinical condition.

measurable_elements.description: Patients receive individualized dietary counseling, activity recommendations, and lifestyle modification guidance relevant to their condition with written materials provided.

objective_elements.description: Discharge education ensures patients understand follow-up care, warning signs, medication regimen, and when to seek emergency care.

measurable_elements.description: Comprehensive discharge education covers medication regimen, follow-up appointments, warning signs, activity restrictions, wound care, and emergency contact information.

objective_elements.description: Patient education materials are standardized, evidence-based, and available in accessible formats.

measurable_elements.description: Standardized patient education materials are developed based on current evidence, reviewed periodically, and available in multiple formats including print, audio, and visual media.

objective_elements.description: The effectiveness of patient education is evaluated through patient feedback, comprehension assessment, and outcome measures.

measurable_elements.description: Patient education effectiveness is measured through teach-back assessments, readmission analysis, patient feedback surveys, and self-management outcome tracking.

standards.title: Rights of Special and Vulnerable Populations
standards.description: The organization identifies and addresses the unique rights and needs of vulnerable and special patient populations.

objective_elements.description: Vulnerable patient populations are identified including children, elderly, differently-abled, mentally ill, and victims of abuse.

measurable_elements.description: The organization identifies vulnerable populations including pediatric, geriatric, differently-abled, mentally ill, and abuse victims with defined protocols for their protection.

objective_elements.description: Pediatric patients have age-appropriate rights protections including parental involvement, assent processes, and child-friendly communication.

measurable_elements.description: Pediatric patient rights include parental involvement in care decisions, age-appropriate assent for older children, child-friendly environments, and protection from unwarranted separation.

objective_elements.description: Elderly patients receive care that addresses their unique vulnerabilities including fall risk, cognitive decline, and polypharmacy concerns.

measurable_elements.description: Geriatric-specific care protocols address fall prevention, cognitive assessment, delirium screening, polypharmacy review, and appropriate discharge planning for elderly patients.

objective_elements.description: Physical accessibility and reasonable accommodations are provided for patients with disabilities.

measurable_elements.description: The organization provides wheelchair access, assistive devices, sign language interpreters, and other accommodations for patients with physical, sensory, or cognitive disabilities.

objective_elements.description: Language interpretation and translation services are available for patients with communication barriers.

measurable_elements.description: Language interpretation services are available for patients who do not speak the predominant language, including access to trained interpreters and translated consent forms.

objective_elements.description: Cultural competency training is provided to staff to ensure equitable and respectful care for diverse patient populations.

measurable_elements.description: Staff receive structured cultural competency training covering implicit bias, cross-cultural communication, and equitable care delivery with training effectiveness assessed.

standards.title: Research Ethics and Patient Protection in Research
standards.description: The organization protects the rights and welfare of patients participating in clinical research through an ethics review process.

objective_elements.description: An institutional ethics committee reviews and approves all clinical research involving human subjects conducted at the organization.

measurable_elements.description: An institutional ethics committee is constituted per regulatory requirements, reviews all research protocols involving human subjects, and provides documented approval before research commencement.

objective_elements.description: Research-specific informed consent is obtained from participants detailing the nature, risks, benefits, and voluntary nature of participation.

measurable_elements.description: Research consent forms include study purpose, procedures, risks, benefits, alternatives, voluntary participation, right to withdraw, confidentiality protections, and investigator contact information.

objective_elements.description: Participants in research are protected from harm with defined safety monitoring, adverse event reporting, and stopping criteria.

measurable_elements.description: Research protocols include participant safety monitoring plans, adverse event reporting mechanisms, data safety monitoring boards where required, and predefined stopping criteria.

objective_elements.description: Research data is maintained confidentially with patient identifiers protected and data security measures in place.

measurable_elements.description: Research data confidentiality is maintained through de-identification, secure storage, restricted access, and data handling agreements with documented security measures.

objective_elements.description: The organization does not allow clinical care to be compromised for patients who decline participation in or withdraw from research.

measurable_elements.description: Policies ensure that patients who decline or withdraw from research continue to receive standard clinical care without discrimination, with staff trained on this principle.

standards.title: Advance Directives and End-of-Life Care Decisions
standards.description: The organization respects and facilitates advance directives and supports patients and families in end-of-life care decision-making.

objective_elements.description: A policy governs the recognition, documentation, and implementation of advance directives in accordance with applicable laws.

measurable_elements.description: The organization has a policy on advance directives covering living wills, healthcare power of attorney, and do-not-resuscitate orders aligned with applicable legal requirements.

objective_elements.description: Advance directives are documented in the patient medical record and communicated to all members of the care team.

measurable_elements.description: When advance directives exist, they are documented prominently in the medical record with alerts in electronic systems and communicated during handoffs and care transitions.

objective_elements.description: Do-not-resuscitate orders follow a defined protocol including physician documentation, patient or family discussion, and periodic review.

measurable_elements.description: DNR orders require documented physician assessment, informed family discussion, patient or surrogate consent, and periodic review with continuation or revocation documented.

objective_elements.description: Staff are trained in end-of-life care communication, ethical decision-making, and palliative care principles.

measurable_elements.description: Clinical staff receive training in breaking bad news, end-of-life communication, palliative care principles, and ethical considerations in withdrawal or withholding of treatment.

objective_elements.description: The organization benchmarks its advance directive and end-of-life care practices against national and international best practices.

measurable_elements.description: The organization benchmarks advance directive compliance rates, palliative care utilization, and end-of-life care quality indicators against national standards and published best practices.
